title: Ginivex-class fanblade starfighter
content:
{{Top|fa|legends=Ginivex-class starfighter/Legends|title=''Ginivex''-class fanblade starfighter}} {{StarshipClass |image=[[File:Fanblade_fighter_TVE.png]] |name=''Ginivex''-class fanblade starfighter |designer= |manufacturer=[[Huppla Pasa Tisc Shipwrights Collective]]{{BuildXWingCite|19|Starfighter Aces}} |line= |model=''Ginivex''-class |type=[[Starfighter]] |cost= |modifier= |sysmods= |length=13.05 [[meter]]s (42 [[Foot|ft]], 11 in)''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia of Starfighters and Other Vehicles]]'' |width=2.25 meters{{EncyclopediaCite|Republic and Separatist Ships (2)}} |height=14.40 meters {{C|sail deployed}} |mass= |max accel= |mglt=125 [[Megalight per hour|MGLT]][[Star Wars Encyclopedia of Starfighters and Other Vehicles (second edition)|''Star Wars Encyclopedia of Starfighters and Other Vehicles'' second edition]] |max speed=1,250 [[Kilometer per hour|kph]] (777 [[Mile per hour|mph]]) |maneuverability= |engine=Air scoop [[Engine|thrusters]] |hyperdrive=[[Hyperdrive|Class 1]] |hdrange= |hdsystem= |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen=[[Deflector shield generator|Equipped]] |hull= |sensor= |target= |navigation= |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures= |armament=[[Laser cannon]]s (2) |complement= |bays= |escapepods= |crew=[[Pilot]] (1){{TCW|Nightsisters (episode)}} |skeleton= |passengers= |capacity= |cargohandling= |consumables= |lifesupport=[[Life support|Equipped]] |communications= |othersystems=*Sail deployment arms (2) *[[Star|Solar]] sail |availability= |role= |firstuse= |retired= |affiliation=*[[Confederacy of Independent Systems]] **[[Asajj Ventress's fleet|Ventress's fleet]] *[[Sith]]{{Databank|asajj-ventress|Asajj Ventress}} }} {{Quote|Anakin, I'm going to need some help.''"
"''Where are you?''"
"[…]'' I'm hit!|Obi-Wan Kenobi informs Anakin Skywalker that his ship has been damaged by Asajj Ventress's fanblade fighter|}} The '''''Ginivex''-class fanblade starfighter''', also known as the '''Ginivex series''' and the '''Geonosian fanblade starfighter''', was a model of [[starfighter]] manufactured by [[Huppla Pasa Tisc Shipwrights Collective]]. It was flown by [[Sith apprentice]] and [[Confederacy of Independent Systems|Separatist]] [[Commanding officer|commander]] [[Asajj Ventress]] during the [[Clone Wars]] against the [[Galactic Republic]]. The [[Starship|ship]] was equipped with a [[Star|solar]] sail, which granted it greater speed and [[Deflector shield|shielding]] at the cost of allowing other ships' [[sensor]]s to more easily detect the fighter. Six fanblade fighters were made for Ventress, one of which she flew during a [[Mission to Cato Neimoidia (Kenobi and Skywalker)|mission]] to the [[planet]] [[Cato Neimoidia]] in [[22 BBY]]. During the mission, Ventress left the starfighter hovering at the end of a sewage tunnel while she confronted the [[Jedi]] [[Anakin Skywalker]] and [[Mill Alibeth]], eventually grabbing Alibeth and leaping aboard the starfighter with her. [[Jedi Master]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]] then damaged the vessel with [[Obi-Wan Kenobi's third lightsaber|his lightsaber]], forcing Ventress to jump inside the sinking vessel as Skywalker saved Alibeth. In [[20 BBY]], Ventress encountered Skywalker again when she used a fanblade starfighter to unsuccessfully try and [[Skirmish on the Halcyon|extract]] the treacherous Republic [[senator]] [[Zast Tri'vak]] from the [[MPO-1400 Purgill-class star cruiser|MPO-1400 ''Purgill''-class star cruiser]] ''[[Halcyon]]''. Ventress also used one of the starfighters in the [[Battle of Sullust]] in the same [[Standard year|year]]. After destroying the [[Venator-class Star Destroyer|Star Destroyer]] ''[[Resolute]]'' with her craft, Ventress [[Starfighter combat|engaged]] Kenobi and Skywalker, although she was forced to crash in the Separatist [[Unidentified Providence-class carrier/destroyer (Battle of Sullust)|command ship's]] [[hangar]]. Despite her master, [[Count of Serenno|Count]] [[Dooku]], turning on Ventress and ordering her remaining forces to destroy the [[flagship]], Ventress escaped aboard her damaged fanblade fighter before the [[Providence-class Dreadnought|cruiser's]] destruction. ==Characteristics== ===Design and equipment=== [[File:Fanblade_cockpit.png|thumb|left|175px|The cockpit of a ''Ginivex''-class fanblade starfighter]] Manufactured by the [[Geonosian]]-run [[Huppla Pasa Tisc Shipwrights Collective]] and constructed on the [[Outer Rim Territories|Outer Rim]] [[planet]]''[[Rise of the Separatists]]'' [[Geonosis]], the ''Ginivex''-class fanblade [[starfighter]]''[[Star Wars: The Visual Encyclopedia]]''—alternatively known as the ''Ginivex''-class fighter, the Ginivex series, Geonosian fanblade starfighter{{SWArchive|url=theclonewars/guide/episode312.html|text=''The Clone Wars'' Episode Guide: Nightsisters}} or fanblade fighter—was a [[Confederacy of Independent Systems]] starfighter that was 13.05 [[meter]]s (42 [[Foot|feet]], 11 inches) long, 2.25 meters wide, 14.40 meters high with sail deployed, and could reach 1,250 [[Kilometer per hour|kilometers per hour]] (777 [[Mile per hour|miles per hour]]) and 125 [[Megalight per hour|megalights per hour]]. It was equipped with [[life support]] systems, air scoop [[Engine|thrusters]], a class 1 [[hyperdrive]], and a [[deflector shield generator]]. Based on [[Dark Lord of the Sith|Sith Lord]] and Confederate [[Head of State]] [[Count of Serenno|Count]] [[Dooku]]'s [[Punworcca 116-class interstellar sloop|''Punworcca 116''-class interstellar sloop]], and thus easily recognizable, the fanblade fighter's fuselage was [[Color|gray]] in color with the [[cockpit]] situated at the front and encased in a [[glass]] and [[transparisteel]]{{ShipsandVehiclesCite|86|History of the Ship}} dome, which was either left clear or colored red,[[Halcyon Legacy 3|''Halcyon Legacy'' 3]] that provided the [[pilot]] a wide field of vision. The cockpit was less spacious than that of a ''Punworcca 116''-class interstellar sloop, though still offered its operator a clear view of their surroundngs. The [[hull]] of the fighter was small, as it was designed for a single occupant. The glass of the cockpit was hinged to allow the pilot to exit[[Hyperspace Stories 5|''Hyperspace Stories'' 5]] or enter the craft. Within the cockpit was a single seat for the pilot, as well as a number of screens used to display information on enemy [[starship]]s. The fighter was flown with the use of a yoke, in the center of which was a [[targeting computer]]. The yoke could also display [[Hologram|holographic]] transmissions, which were colored red. ===Crimson blade=== [[File:Ginivex-profile.png|thumb|right|250px|The Ginivex had a crimson red solar sail similar to shield projection technology.]] The ship was equipped with a large, vertically aligned [[Star|solar]] sail, which appeared as a translucent red semicircle and connected to a retractable boom, which could be folded inward for when the craft was in flight or landing. The fanblade fighter was also equipped with a backup sail, which could be deployed in place of the primary one. [[Engineer]]s from Huppla Pasa Tisc Shipwrights Collective created an updated sail for Ventress' fighter after Dooku requested a solar sail for his personal vessel. Similar to [[deflector shield]] projection technology, the solar sail on the ''Ginivex''-class formed a fan of energy that captured [[radiation]] from the surrounding area and redirected it to the starfighter's shield systems, [[weapon]]s, and engines. Despite the sail having no impact on the starfighter's performance and its ability to be unaffected by most weapons, an ion weapon could force the solar sail to recharge and then redeploy. As a safety measure, the sail had to be deactivated in atmospheric flight and before a [[hyperspace]] jump, since the structures that held the sail were not rugged enough. Differentiating it from other starfighters, and most starships, was the crimson solar sail fan that meant that the fighter could be easily spotted. Although it was obtrusive, the effect of the ebullient blade on opponents outweighed the starfighter's lack of stealth. At each tip of the solar sail was a [[laser cannon]], which would rotate to face forward when the sail was retracted. The [[cannon]]s could aim independently and in combination to cover a full range of 360 degrees. Their firepower was capable of piercing the hull of a [[Venator-class Star Destroyer|''Venator''-class Star Destroyer]]. ==Role== A rare and [[Currency|expensive]] ship, the [[starfighter combat]]-capable ''Ginivex''-class fanblade fighter was incredibly fast, thanks to its solar sail. When deployed, the sail would better spread the ship's deflector shield pattern, thereby strengthening its shielding, although the resulting energy output made the starfighter more detectable by other vessels' [[sensor]]s. When flown by a [[Force-sensitive|Force-user]], the fighter could perform turns and flips, which showcased the joint capability of the craft and its pilot. ==History== ===Mission to Cato Neimoidia=== {{Quote|Wait. Stay behind me. Something's not right. I think I've seen that ship before.|Anakin Skywalker spots Ventress's fanblade starfighter|[[Brotherhood (novel)|''Brotherhood'']]}} [[File:Mission-to-Cato-Neimoidia-final.png|thumb|left|300px|Asajj Ventress's ''Ginivex''-class fanblade starfighter was damaged during a confrontation with Jedi Anakin Skywalker and Obi-Wan Kenobi on Cato Neimoidia.]] Only six ''Ginivex''-class fanblade starfighters were constructed for [[Asajj Ventress]], a Force-sensitive [[Dathomirian]] loyal to the [[Sith]] who was [[Sith apprentice|apprenticed]] to [[Count]] Dooku. A [[Commanding officer|commander]] in the Confederacy of Independent Systems, Ventress brought one of her fanblade fighters with her to the planet [[Cato Neimoidia]] during the [[Clone Wars]] in [[22 BBY]],''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]'' dates the events of [[Brotherhood (novel)|''Brotherhood'']] to [[22 BBY]]. while acting as a Separatist [[emissary]] in the [[Mission to Cato Neimoidia (Kenobi and Skywalker)|investigation]] of the [[Cadesura disaster|bombing]] of the [[Cadesura district]] of the [[city]] of [[Zarra]]. Ventress framed the [[Galactic Republic]]'s emissary, [[Jedi Master]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]], for the [[bomb]]ing, and after he was rescued from the [[Neimoidian]] authorities by his allies [[Anakin Skywalker]], [[Mill Alibeth]], and [[R2-D2]], she used the craft to cut off their attempt to escape Cato Neimoidia. Ventress positioned the fighter hovering above a great drop outside an outlet to one of Zarra's sewer tunnels, which Skywalker, Alibeth, and R2-D2 were moving through. After Skywalker sensed the starfighter, which he noted seemed familiar—despite being initially confused by its design—Ventress used one of its blaster cannons to collapse the [[duracrete]] tunnel exit. The Sith then confronted Skywalker in the darkened tunnel, but after the Jedi bested Ventress in a [[Lightsaber duel|duel]], she grabbed Alibeth and jumped through an opening Skywalker had made in the rubble at the tunnel exit. Landing on her craft with her captive caused the hovering vessel to dip slightly with the impact, sinking into the fog beneath it for a few moments before stabilizing. This was enough to distract Ventress, and Skywalker used the distraction to [[Telekinesis|pull]] Alibeth from the Sith's grasp using [[the Force]]. Before he could pull her back safely to ground, Ventress stood atop the fanblade's cockpit while pulling back on Alibeth with the Force, leaving the [[Gender|girl]] hovering in midair over a great drop between the ship and the sewer. Kenobi, who had arrived above the sewer, threw [[Obi-Wan Kenobi's third lightsaber|his lightsaber]] down at the vital parts of the starfighter's hull, piercing it and causing a puff of smoke, followed by a short burst of [[Fire|flame]], to emerge from the craft. As key systems failed, the fanblade starfighter gradually began to descend, forcing Ventress to release Alibeth and let Skywalker pull her to safety. The Sith then scrambled inside the cockpit as the vessel gained more downward momentum, allowing the Jedi to escape and flee from Cato Neimoidia. ===Hunting an artifact=== {{Quote|I can feel its energy… ''Kashyyyk''… no… it was there… but… yes… perhaps…|Asajj Ventress senses the presence of the artifact on Naboo|}} [[File:VentressDeparts-HyperspaceStories5.png|thumb|right|300px|After sensing the item she seeks is on Naboo, Asajj Ventress departs from the Moon of Staggec aboard one of her ''Ginivex''-class fighters.]] After she successfully [[Capture of Outpost 716|captured]] [[Outpost 716]], Ventress was [[Hunt for a Jedi artifact|dispatched]] by Dooku to find a [[Unidentified Jedi artifact|Jedi artifact]], which he promised they could use to overthrow his own [[Sith Master]], [[Darth Sidious]]. Pointed to the ruins of a [[Jedi Temple (Moon of Staggec)|Jedi Temple]] on the [[Moon of Staggec]] in the Outer Rim, Ventress arrived aboard one of her ''Ginivex''-class fanblade starfighters and landed in a town outside of the temple. However, she [[Ambush on the moon of Staggec|found]] nothing within but traces of the object's presence in the Force. Returning to her fighter, she was ambushed by waiting Republic [[clone trooper]]s, who had been informed of her location by [[Supreme Chancellor]] Sheev Palpatine,[[Hyperspace Stories 5|''Hyperspace Stories'' 5]] secretly Sidious himself. Ventress quickly cut the [[Cloning|clones]] down with her [[Asajj Ventress's lightsabers|twin lightsabers]]. Ventress departed the moon aboard her Ginivex after sensing the artifact was on the planet [[Naboo]] in the [[Mid Rim Territories]], where she [[Mission to Naboo (Asajj Ventress)|journeyed]] aboard her Ginivex. She traveled to the [[Naboo (people)|Naboo]] capital of [[Theed]], landing her starfighter slightly outside the city before venturing in. Within, she found Kenobi and Skywalker were present and attempted to grab a [[Viiveenn's doll|Wookiee doll]] from a [[Unidentified Gungan (Viiveenn's doll)|Gungan child]]: Ventress sensed the artifact was within the [[doll]], where it had been hidden by the [[Wookiee]] [[Senator]] [[Yarua]].[[Hyperspace Stories 1|''Hyperspace Stories'' 1]] After escaping Skywalker and Kenobi, Ventress decided against stealing the doll from the child upon realizing Dooku did not truly value her. Ventress thus departed Naboo aboard her starfighter and used it to return to [[Count Dooku's solar sailer|Dooku's solar sailer]], where she claimed she had failed to find the artifact, much to Dooku's anger. ===''Halcyon'' escape=== {{Quote|Is that the proximity alarm I hear?''"
"Er''… it was nothing, sir. Just a glitch.''"
"''Okay. Carry on.|The crew of the ''Halcyon'' dismiss the arrival of Ventress in her fanblade starfighter|}} [[File:VentressSpaceJump-HalcyonLegacy3.png|thumb|left|300px|Asajj Ventress leaps from the ''Halcyon'' into the cockpit of her fanblade starfighter.]] In [[20 BBY]],''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]'' dates the flashback events of [[Halcyon Legacy 3|''Halcyon Legacy'' 3]] to [[20 BBY]]. Ventress used a fanblade starfighter to board the ''[[Halcyon]]'', an [[MPO-1400 Purgill-class star cruiser|MPO-1400 ''Purgill''-class star cruiser]] operated by the [[Hutt Clan|Hutts]], while trying to [[Skirmish on the Halcyon|extract]] the treacherous Republic Senator [[Zast Tri'vak]]. The [[cruiser]]'s [[Proximity sensor|proximity alarm]] sounded when she entered its [[hangar]], but it was dismissed as a glitch; however, Skywalker, who was on board with Senator [[Padmé Amidala]] to keep an [[eye]] on Tri'vak, sensed her arrival. The pair ended up dueling once again, but Tri'vak ultimately offered to betray both the Republic and the Separatists to the Hutts, causing Ventress to [[Death|kill]] him. Having remotely activated the fanblade starfighter to open its cockpit hatch and leave the ''Halcyon''{{'s}} hangar, Ventress then used her [[lightsaber]]s to shatter one of the ''Halcyon''{{'s}} [[viewport]]s and leap into the [[vacuum]] of [[Realspace|space]], where her starfighter was waiting for her. As Skywalker sealed the breach, Ventress escaped in her fighter. ===Battle of Sullust=== {{Quote|I've got her.|Anakin Skywalker prepares to fire at Asajj Ventress's fanblade fighter during the Battle of Sullust|}} In the same [[Standard year|year]],''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]'' Ventress flew one of her fanblade fighters, the [[Banshee (starfighter)|''Banshee'']],''[[Star Wars Villainous: Power of the Dark Side]]'' against the Republic during the [[Battle of Sullust]]. With the aid of [[Trident group one|two]] [[Trident group two|groups]] of [[droid tri-fighter]]s from [[Asajj Ventress's fleet|her fleet]], Ventress took aim at Skywalker's [[flagship]], the ''[[Resolute]]'', dealing substantial damage to the Star Destroyer's [[Bridge (starship)|bridge]] and resulting in the cruiser's destruction.{{SWArchive|url=theclonewars/guide/episode312.html|text=''The Clone Wars'' Episode Guide: Nightsisters}} [[File:BattleOfSullust-VentressAttack.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Asajj Ventress pilots one of her fanblade fighters during the Battle of Sullust.]] Ventress and her tri-fighters then targeted Kenobi and Skywalker directly, both of whom were piloting their [[Delta-7B Aethersprite-class light interceptor|Delta-7B ''Aethersprite''-class light interceptors]]. After the Dathomirian damaged [[Obi-Wan Kenobi's Delta-7B Aethersprite-class light interceptor|Kenobi's craft]] with her fanblade fighter's lower [[laser]] cannon, the two Jedi split up, leaving Skywalker to contend with the [[droid starfighter]]s while Ventress gave chase to Kenobi. As the Jedi Master approached the Separatist [[Unidentified Providence-class carrier/destroyer (Battle of Sullust)|flagship]], Ventress scored a second shot on Kenobi's ship, forcing him to head for the [[command ship]]'s main hangar in order to land. Ventress stayed on Kenobi's tail, but she was intercepted by Skywalker, who damaged the hull of the ''Banshee'' and forced her to crash in the hangar. ===Ventress betrayed=== {{Quote|You have failed me for the last time. You are no longer my apprentice. And now, you shall die.|Dooku, speaking through a hologram projected by the fanblade fighter's communication systems|}} [[File:Fanblade-wreck_Raider.png|thumb|left|300px|The ''Raider'' retrieves the wreckage of Asajj Ventress's fanblade fighter.]] With her ship aslant in the hangar with a disabled solar sail, Ventress communicated with Dooku via hologram in the fighter's cockpit, requesting the Count's assistance. However, Dooku—who had been instructed by Darth Sidious to kill Ventress, fearing that the Dathomirian had been growing too powerful in the Force—informed her that she had failed him and was no longer his apprentice. Embittered, Ventress emerged from the cockpit and briefly dueled Kenobi and Skywalker as Dooku ordered the remaining Separatist forces to destroy the flagship. After a [[Hyena-class Droid Fighter/Bomber|''Hyena''-class Droid Bomber]] crashed into the hangar and exploded, Kenobi, Skywalker, and Ventress each returned to their own craft and narrowly escaped before the command ship's destruction. Following the battle, Ventress was found adrift and unconscious aboard her fanblade fighter—sustained by its life-support systems—by a crew of [[scavenger]]s, who brought the ship aboard their salvage [[freighter]], the [[Raider (salvage freighter)|''Raider'']].{{SW|url=series/clone-wars/nightsisters-trivia-gallery|text="Nightsisters" Trivia Gallery}} {{C|Slide 12}} Her fighter's resilience enabled Ventress' survival. After placing the fighter in the freighter's hold, the crew removed the cockpit dome and revitalized Ventress, who used the Force to [[Force choke|strangle]] them soon after. With her fanblade fighter still aboard, Ventress took control of the ''Raider'' and piloted it to her [[homeworld]] of [[Dathomir]].{{Databank|asajj-ventress|Asajj Ventress}} ==Behind the scenes== ===Appearance=== {{Quote|Master, are you all right?''"
"''I am now. With your help and Master Ma'kis, we can blow that witch out of the stars.|Ahsoka Tano and Anakin Skywalker, preparing to chase Asajj Ventress's fanblade fighter in a deleted scene for "Nightsisters"|{{HomeVideoCite|set=Star Wars: The Clone Wars The Complete Season Three|episode=Nightsisters (episode)}}}} [[File:Fanblade_cockpitTCW.png|thumb|right|250px|Concept art of the fanblade fighter's cockpit]] The ''Ginivex''-class fanblade starfighter first appeared in the current ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]] in "[[Nightsisters (episode)|Nightsisters]]," the twelfth episode of the animated television series ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]''{{'}} [[The Clone Wars: Season Three|third season]], which premiered on [[January 7]], [[2011]]. The [[Ginivex-class starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] was originally made for the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] animated television micro-series ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'', where it first appeared in "[[Chapter 7 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)|Chapter 7]]" of the show,{{CW|7}} which first aired on [[November 17]], [[2003]].{{TVcom|url=shows/star-wars-clone-wars/chapter-vii-276613|text=Star Wars: Clone Wars - Season 1, Episode 7: Chapter VII}} [[Mike Chen (author)|Mike Chen]]—the author of the [[2022]] novel [[Brotherhood (novel)|''Brotherhood'']], in which Ventress and one of her ''Ginivex''-class starfighters appear—wrote his interactions between Anakin Skywalker and Ventress as if the ''Clone Wars'' micro-series were still canon, simply assuming that Skywalker never saw Ventress's face during their [[Duel on Yavin 4|first meeting]].{{Twitter|mikechenwriter|status/1674180634078597121|[[Mike Chen (author)|Mike Chen]]|date=2023-06-28|quote=Note that my Anakin/Ventress encounter at the end of the book is written to work with [[Genndy Tartakovsky|Genddy]] [''sic'']! Anakin says he's seen her ship before and Ventress knows who he is. This assumes that Yavin was dark, fast, and rainy, so Anakin knows he was attacked on Yavin but not by who.|archivedate=20230717232827|nolive=1}} To hint that they have encountered each other before, Skywalker is noted to recognize her fanblade starfighter.{{Twitter|mikechenwriter|status/1674181911986728963|[[Mike Chen (author)|Mike Chen]]|date=2023-06-28|quote=Anakin says he recognizes her ship and he's dealt with Dooku's agents before. Ventress just laughs at him and clearly knows who he is. We didn't want too much more expo to bog the story down but those are the clues.|archivedate=20230717233214|nolive=1}} As the design for [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] herself originated from unused concept art made for the{{DB|starship|fanbladestarfighter|Geonosian fanblade starfighter}} [[2002]] [[prequel trilogy]] film,{{Film|II}} [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']], that depicted a warrior reminiscent of [[Wikipedia:Japan|Japan's]] [[Wikipedia:Samurai|samurai]], ''Star Wars: Clone Wars'' Art Director [[Paul Rudish]] looked to that nation for inspiration on Ventress's starfighter. Rudish based the ship's distinctive shape on the [[Wikipedia:Hand fan#Japanese hand fan|sensu]], a type of Japanese folding fan. One of Ventress's ''Ginivex''-class fanblade starfighters would later be included on a Ventress-themed variant cover for the [[Doctor Aphra (2020) 35|thirty-fifth issue]] of the [[2020]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series [[Star Wars: Doctor Aphra (2020)|''Star Wars: Doctor Aphra'']], which was released on [[August 23]], [[2023]]. The cover was drawn by [[Giuseppe Camuncoli]] and depicts a fanblade flying nearby the [[B'omarr Order Monastery]] of{{SW|url=news/swce-2023-marvel|text=SWCE 2023: Marvel Reveals ''Star Wars: Dark Droids'' Horror Event and More}} the [[Wild Space]] planet [[Teth]], which was used by Ventress early in the Clone Wars in a [[Battle of Teth|Separatist plot]] to frame the Jedi Order in the kidnapping of [[Rotta]] the [[Hutt]]let.[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (film)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' film]] ===Naming and contradictions=== [[File:Fanblade.jpg|thumb|left|175px|The ''Ginivex''-class starfighter in ''Star Wars: Clone Wars'']] In Legends, the ship was identified as the ''Ginivex''-class [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]], as well as the Geonosian fanblade starfighter, in the now-defunct [[StarWars.com]] [[Databank (original)|Databank]]. In the accompanying StarWars.com [[The Clone Wars Episode Guides|Episode Guide]] for "Nightsisters," the craft was canonically referred to as the Geonosian fanblade starfighter,{{SW|url=series/clone-wars/nightsisters-trivia-gallery|text="Nightsisters" Trivia Gallery}} {{C|Slide 8}} and the [[2017]] canon reference book ''[[Star Wars: The Visual Encyclopedia]]'' provided the name ''Ginivex''-class fanblade starfighter. A deleted scene for "Nightsisters" has [[Ahsoka Tano]], Anakin Skywalker's [[Padawan|Jedi apprentice]],{{Databank|anakin-skywalker|Anakin Skywalker}} disable Ventress's fanblade fighter during the Battle of Sullust; however, Tano is absent from the final episode, and Skywalker sends Ventress crashing instead. While the fighter's cockpit is completely clear in "Nightsisters," the rendering of the fanblade fighter seen in ''Star Wars: The Visual Encyclopedia'' shows the ship's cockpit window as tinted red, and the [[Halcyon Legacy 3|third issue]] of the comic book miniseries ''[[Star Wars: Halcyon Legacy]]''—which was released on [[May 11]], 2022, written by [[Ethan Sacks]], and illustrated by [[Will Sliney]]{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/96846/star_wars_the_halcyon_legacy_2022_3|text=Star Wars: The Halcyon Legacy (2022) #3}}—depicted the ship with a red cockpit. The [[2018]] reference book ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia of Starfighters and Other Vehicles]]'', written by [[Landry Q. Walker]], gives the fanblade fighter's length as 13.1 meters, while the [[Star Wars: Build Your Own X-Wing 19|nineteenth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Build Your Own X-Wing]]'' magazine, published later that year, and the [[Star Wars Encyclopedia (series)|''Star Wars Encyclopedia'']] booklet "[[Republic and Separatist Ships (2)]]," published on [[January 28]], 2022,{{Altaya|lang=fr|url=livres-bd/encyclopedie-star-wars|text=Official Star Wars Encyclopedia}} lists the fighter's length as 13.05 meters. As the latter sources were published more recently, this article defers to their measurements. ==Appearances== *[[Brotherhood (novel)|''Brotherhood'']] {{Ab|Brotherhood (audiobook)}} *[[Hyperspace Stories 5|''Hyperspace Stories'' 5]] *{{TCW|Nightsisters (episode)}} {{1st}} *{{TCW|Monster}} {{Wreck}} {{Flash}} *[[Doctor Aphra (2020) 35|''Doctor Aphra'' (2020) 35]] {{C|Variant cover only}} *[[Halcyon Legacy 3|''Halcyon Legacy'' 3]] {{Flash}} ==Sources== *{{SWArchive|url=theclonewars/guide/episode312.html|text=''The Clone Wars'' Episode Guide: Nightsisters}} {{1stID|Geonosian fanblade starfighter}} *{{SW|url=series/clone-wars/nightsisters-episode-guide|text="Nightsisters" Episode Guide | The Clone Wars}} *{{SWE|characters|asajjventress|Asajj Ventress}} *''[[Star Wars: The Visual Encyclopedia]]'' {{1stID|''Ginivex''-class fanblade starfighter}} *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia of Starfighters and Other Vehicles]]'' {{1stID|fanblade fighter}} *{{BuildXWingCite|19|Starfighter Aces}} *''[[Rise of the Separatists]]'' *{{EncyclopediaCite|Republic and Separatist Ships (2)}} {{1stID|Ginivex series and ''Ginivex''-class fighter}} *{{SW|url=news/marvel-star-wars-halcyon-legacy-3-exclusive-preview|text=A Private Moment for Anakin and Padmé in Marvel's ''Star Wars: Halcyon Legacy'' #3 – Exclusive Preview}} *''[[Star Wars Villainous: Power of the Dark Side]]'' *{{SW|url=news/swce-2023-marvel|text=SWCE 2023: Marvel Reveals ''Star Wars: Dark Droids'' Horror Event and More}} {{Po}}
